---

## 📌 Feature Name

> A precise, system-consumable title.  
> _Example: Dynamic CEFT Tensor Collapse Visualizer_

---

## 1. 🧠 Feature Summary

Describe the **essence** of the feature in one powerful, descriptive paragraph.  
What does it add to the system’s behavior or usability? What theory or ontology does it express?

---

## 2. 🧩 Unified Theory Alignment

| Theory Element                | Alignment | Justification                                                  |
|------------------------------|-----------|----------------------------------------------------------------|
| CEFT (Curvature Entropy)     | ✅ / ❌    | Does it manipulate or visualize entropy flow or curvature?     |
| FDT (Force Differentiation)  | ✅ / ❌    | Does it involve emergence, separation, or manipulation of forces? |
| SDI (Symmetry Decay Index)   | ✅ / ❌    | Does it relate to loss of symmetry or ordering over time?       |
| SRI (Selection Resonance)    | ✅ / ❌    | Does it affect state stability, resonance chains, or collapse?  |
| Absolute Entity Consistency  | ✅ / ❌    | Is the feature abstracted within the bounds of Absolute Nothingness? |

---

## 3. 🔍 User-Level Use Case

Who benefits from this and how?  
Frame it in terms of an **AxAbsEnt user role**:

> _“As a quantum theorist using the AxAbsEnt simulation backend, I want to observe in real time the differential behavior of weak-force curvature collapse tensors under transfinite chain resonance failures, so that I can isolate phase instability conditions at high-dimensional spacetime inflection zones.”_

---

## 4. 🧪 Target Modules

Which parts of the system would this affect or integrate with?

| Module                  | Type of Change           | Notes                                  |
|-------------------------|---------------------------|----------------------------------------|
| `src/core/`             | Logic / Operator          | New operator logic                     |
| `src/forces/`           | Emergence / Extraction    | Expanded decomposition schema          |
| `src/simulation/`       | Simulation Kernel         | Adds tensor evolution pipeline         |
| `src/visualization/`    | UI / Visual Interface     | New entropy map or tensor viewer       |
| `api/routes/`           | API Endpoint              | REST access for generated behavior     |
| `web/src/components/`   | Frontend Interaction      | React integration, controls, graphs    |
| `docs/tutorials/`       | Documentation + Tutorial  | Theory-to-practice user instruction    |

---

## 5. 📐 Functional Requirements

List the **functional deliverables** of the feature.

- [ ] Must generate curvature-entropy maps with selectable resolution
- [ ] Must expose `/forces/curvature-map` endpoint via API
- [ ] Must compute tensor projection under variable SDI thresholds
- [ ] Must export simulation outputs in `.hdf5` format
- [ ] Must be visualizable as interactive 3D fields in web dashboard

---

## 6. ⚖️ Security, Compliance & Regulatory Checks

| Domain                   | Risk/Impact         | Notes                                                   |
|--------------------------|---------------------|----------------------------------------------------------|
| Data Privacy (GDPR)      | Low / Medium / High | Any user-uploaded input processed/stored?               |
| AI Act Classification    | Low / Medium / High | Will the feature output be used for autonomous systems? |
| Model Execution Security | Low / Medium / High | Are kernels sandboxed? Access to GPU memory managed?    |
| Logging & Auditing       | Required / Optional | Are traces of this feature captured for compliance?     |

---

## 7. 🔁 Integration Phases

Break down the feature into modular PR milestones:

- `PR1`: Define operator and unit test interface
- `PR2`: Integrate with simulation pipeline
- `PR3`: Expose API route and add REST contract
- `PR4`: Add visualization layer and frontend interaction
- `PR5`: Write documentation, update notebooks, finalize test coverage

---

## 8. 🧠 Validation Strategy

How will success be verified?

- [ ] Unit tests pass with CI
- [ ] Simulation output matches theoretical expectation
- [ ] Visualizer renders expected entropy patterns
- [ ] Cross-module integration tested in `/notebooks/12_complete_analysis_pipeline.ipynb`

---

## 9. 📖 Documentation & Outreach

- [ ] Add Sphinx docs in `docs/concepts/` and `docs/tutorials/`
- [ ] Add interactive notebook in `notebooks/`
- [ ] Update `README.md` if public interface changes
- [ ] Generate visual poster/presentation if feature is major
